accelerated, certainly, with with folks like like at the scale of Cushman in part accelerated because of the doj components of this, but also just the automation, the granularity, the data, the frequency of the data.
As we've gotten to know more companies in the space, and they've gotten to rely more on our market data.
We've had a number of customers who have over the last year started to ask us if we can start to put something together that would that would utilize the data we have in a revenue management tool.

Sometimes those people are moving off of air to try and build models on their own.
Sometimes they're trying to find a new vendor.
It feels like just about everybody shopping in one way or another.

We have.

One thing.

But I still want data on my competitors.
And I'm already using Apartment IQ to gut check and understand the reasoning for the recommendations the system is using.
Why am I having to jump around to do that.

We started working on this product in earnest late last year.

I'm just pulling up a demo account.

The Market Survey tab for apartment IQ.

So we.
this this system as it is today is not touching any property management software.
So this is all powered off the apartment.
IQ data.

If you access my private data as long as you're not sharing it with others, and then combine that with the public competitive data you have.

How I want.

So the model that's been built is the underlying model is a machine learning model.
And ultimately I don't know how much you've gotten into this.

But ultimately, these machine learning models basically create a list of a list of metrics that can help predict both how many leases you might sign at a given price point, and then other additional price points over the given timeframe.

And so it's a machine learning model that, and about half of the metrics that are informing this model, and the predictiveness of this model are actually related to what's happening in your market, and the other half relate to just your property.
Things like, how many leases have you signed in the last 14 days?
How much exposure does property have?
etc?
And so that's what's going on behind the scenes.
But functionally, it's working the way that you might expect it to.
You can set a sustainable occupancy or budgeted occupancy for the property which you can see in gray here, and the system is looking at.
What's your current occupancy?
And then you can set a timeframe for that occupancy.
The most common in our system is a 56 day timeframe, because we're working off of only data that is available on your website.
And usually folks do a 60 day notice.
And so we'll be able to see everything that's coming up in the next 60 days, and the model can work off of that.
So what the model is doing is it's saying, Okay, where are you today?
Where are you trending in the timeframe you select.
You could select 28 days, 56 days, or 84 day timelines, timeframes, though, that that could be customized and the model is guessing at the current price points.
You're at.
How many leases and how many applications are you going to sign in that given timeframe?
And it does break it down.
Applications versus leases.
It uses your historical application to lease, conversion rate to calculate what that looks like.
You can see the system is already starting to make some recommendations.

And so what the system is doing is you can optimize it to say, Hey, I want to hit a budget occupancy for my property level, and then we can flow that down, or you can say, I want to hit a budgeted occupancy for my bed count level, and we can optimize from there.
But ultimately the model is gonna work exactly the same, it's just what is it optimizing for?

Seasonality is such a big component of this, even down to the fact of, if you're pricing historically, you've been pricing at the 50th percentile, and your leasing velocity has been X.

It's logical, and I think a lot of folks think about it as well, if I move to the 70th percentile of that market, I can probably predict what that leasing velocity is going to be, and it's going to be different.
So we have found a pretty substantial difference in the actual recommendations of the system that come out as a result of being able to use that competitive data.
